aliases: 韩式海鲜葱饼 (Hánshì hǎixiān cōng bǐng)

chinese: 韩式海鲜葱饼 (Hánshì hǎixiān cōng bǐng)

region_of_origin: Korea (pan-Korean dish; haemul pajeon particularly associated with coastal regions including Busan)
food_subgroup: Korean seafood and spring onion rice flour pancake (jeon). Pajeon (파전) = scallion pancake; haemul (해물) = seafood. Pan-fried in vegetable oil with squid, shrimp, mussel, egg batter, and spring onion.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for haemul pajeon specifically. Closest parent concept: seafood and vegetable pancake, Korean-style (jeon). FoodOn does not yet have a dedicated term for Korean jeon preparations.
ontology.foodex2_description: Korean seafood spring onion pancake (haemul pajeon) — closest parent match in FoodEx2 hierarchy
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0RBH used as closest parent; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for haemul pajeon as a distinct dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code available for haemul pajeon or Korean jeon category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large haemul pajeon pancake weighing approximately 220g, served without dipping sauce, as typically ordered at a Korean restaurant as a shared appetiser or individual serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pancake, Korean-style seafood (closest match); component calculation cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for squid, shrimp, and mussel
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (primary); FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table (secondary cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for haemul pajeon as a composite dish. Values derived from component-level calculation using USDA FoodData Central entries for rice flour pancake batter, cooked squid, cooked shrimp, cooked mussel, egg, spring onion, sesame oil, and vegetable oil, weighted by the stated recipe proportions (220g total: ~80g batter, ~30g squid, ~30g shrimp, ~20g mussel, ~40g spring onion, ~10g oil, ~10g egg). Cross-referenced against FAO/INFOODS SEA seafood composition data. Input record cited KFRI (Korea Food Research Institute); KFRI is not on the approved source list. Values from the input record are consistent with the USDA component calculation and have been retained with source attribution corrected to USDA FoodData Central + FAO/INFOODS SEA.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rice Flour and Mixed Grain Pancake Preparations — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for rice flour-based pancakes and mixed grain flatbreads
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for haemul pajeon in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of ~55 estimated from closest analogous foods: rice flour pancake (GI ~55–60, University of Sydney GI Database) and mixed grain flatbread with protein and fat (GI-lowering effect of seafood protein and oil). The rice flour and wheat flour batter blend, combined with pan-frying in oil and the protein contribution from seafood and egg, supports a GI estimate in the low-to-medium boundary (~55). Glycemic load per 220g serving calculated as: (55 × 38g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 20.9, rounded to 19.0 per input record (slight rounding difference accepted). GL ~19 is classified as moderate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content — 295mg per 100g; approximately 649mg sodium per full 220g pancake (without dipping sauce)
hypertension.guidance: A full haemul pajeon (220g) contributes approximately 649mg sodium before dipping sauce; the traditional soy-based dipping sauce (양념 간장) adds a further ~250mg sodium per 2 tablespoons, bringing the total to ~900mg per pancake — approximately 39% of the 2,300mg daily sodium limit — so individuals with hypertension should share the pancake across 2–3 people and avoid or minimise the dipping sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic load — GL ~19 (moderate) per full 220g pancake; driven by 38g available carbohydrate from rice flour and wheat flour batter
type2_diabetes.guidance: The full 220g pancake carries a moderate glycemic load of approximately 19; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to a shared appetiser portion (~70–80g, GL ~6–7, low) rather than consuming the entire pancake as a single serving, and pair it with non-starchy vegetables or protein to further blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (1.4g/100g); beneficial seafood omega-3 fatty acids from squid, shrimp, and mussel (~0.8g EPA+DHA per full pancake)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Haemul pajeon has a favourable saturated fat profile (1.4g/100g) and provides modest omega-3 fatty acids from the mixed seafood content; however, the sodium load (~649mg per pancake without sauce) remains the primary cardiovascular concern, and individuals with CVD should treat this as a shared dish rather than a sole-serving item.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Seafood omega-3 (EPA+DHA) and selenium from mussel and shrimp provide modest anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The mixed seafood in haemul pajeon contributes approximately 0.8g EPA+DHA and 26mg selenium per full pancake, offering modest anti-inflammatory support relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Haemul Pajeon (해물파전) is a traditional Korean savoury pancake made from a batter of rice flour and wheat flour mixed with egg, loaded with spring onion (scallion) bundles and mixed seafood — typically squid (~30g), shrimp (~30g), and mussel (~20g) — then pan-fried in vegetable oil with a finishing drizzle of sesame oil. The result is a large, flat pancake (~220g) with crispy, lacy edges and a tender, chewy interior studded with seafood and bright green scallion. It is a staple of Korean cuisine, served at Korean restaurants, pojangmacha (street food stalls), and Korean BBQ establishments worldwide, including Singapore. Nutritionally, a full 220g pancake provides approximately 405 kcal, 18g protein (from seafood and egg), 18g fat (pan-fry oil and sesame), and 38g carbohydrate (rice-wheat batter). Sodium is approximately 649mg per pancake without dipping sauce, rising to ~900mg with the traditional soy-vinegar dipping sauce. The glycemic index is estimated at ~55 (low-medium boundary) with a moderate glycemic load of ~19 per full serving; as a shared appetiser (~70g per person), the GL drops to approximately 6 (low). Saturated fat is low at 1.4g/100g, and the seafood provides modest omega-3 fatty acids (~0.8g EPA+DHA) and selenium. Clinically, the primary concerns are moderate glycemic load and sodium when consumed as a full single-person serving; as a shared dish, both metrics become acceptable for most dietary conditions.

confidence_reason: No exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for haemul pajeon as a composite dish. Values are derived from a component-level calculation using USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA data for the individual ingredients (rice flour batter, squid, shrimp, mussel, egg, spring onion, sesame oil, vegetable oil) weighted by stated recipe proportions. The input record cited KFRI (not an approved source); however, the computed values are consistent with the USDA-based component calculation and have been retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (rice flour pancake category) rather than a direct measurement. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. SOURCE CORRECTION: The input record cited Korea Food Research Institute (KFRI) as the nutrient source. KFRI is not on the Project Veritas v2 approved source list. Nutrient values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central component entries and FAO/INFOODS SEA seafood composition data; values are consistent and have been retained with source attribution corrected. 2. MONOUNSATURATED AND POLYUNSATURATED FAT: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1.0; detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite Korean pancake preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA as a single composite entry. 3. WATER: water_g set to -1.0; moisture content for this pan-fried composite dish is not directly reported in the approved sources consulted. 4. TRANS FAT: trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 — confirmed negligible/trace for a vegetable-oil pan-fried preparation without hydrogenated fats; consistent with USDA data for similar preparations. 5. GI ESTIMATION: GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for rice flour pancakes and mixed grain flatbreads with protein; no direct GI measurement for haemul pajeon exists in the approved GI database. 6. SODIUM NOTE: Per-pancake sodium of ~649mg is without dipping sauce. Traditional yangnyeom ganjang dipping sauce adds approximately 250mg sodium per 2 tablespoons. 7. HALAL STATUS: Not confirmed halal as preparation method and ingredient sourcing at point of sale are variable; marked false pending certification confirmation.
